name: Scalekit Enterprise SSO API
what_it_does: Enterprise SSO and directory sync API that adds SAML/OIDC single sign-on and SCIM provisioning to SaaS products — allowing B2B SaaS companies to offer enterprise SSO without building it in-house.
use_cases: Adding SAML and OIDC enterprise SSO to B2B SaaS products via simple API integration
Automating user provisioning and deprovisioning with SCIM directory sync
Managing enterprise customer SSO configurations programmatically per organization
Enabling magic links and social login alongside enterprise SSO from one API
Syncing users from Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace to application databases
not_for: Consumer-facing auth (use Auth0 or Clerk for B2C)
Internal workforce identity management (use Okta or Azure AD)
Simple username/password auth without SSO requirements
best_when: A B2B SaaS product needs to add enterprise SSO quickly without building SAML/OIDC from scratch, supporting customer IT teams managing identity.
avoid_when: You need B2C authentication or internal employee identity management — Scalekit is purpose-built for B2B SaaS SSO.

auth.notes: Client credentials OAuth2 for server-to-server API access. API keys for management operations. Organization-scoped tokens for per-customer configuration. Webhook signatures for payload verification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 3 SSO connections, 100 active users
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $49/month; Scale and Enterprise with custom pricing
pricing.notes: Developer-friendly free tier for integration testing. Per-connection pricing model for production. Competitive with WorkOS for B2B SSO. No per-seat pricing — predictable costs.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits documented per endpoint; generous for auth flows

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Strong developer documentation with code samples in multiple languages. OpenAPI spec available. Error responses include error codes and human-readable messages. Webhook delivery for SSO events and SCIM operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Organization and connection creation is idempotent by external ID. User sync operations via SCIM are idempotent. Safe to retry configuration operations.
known_agent_gotchas: SAML/OIDC configuration requires customer IT admin action — not fully automatable
Directory sync (SCIM) events are async — webhook handling required for real-time sync
Organization must exist before SSO connection can be configured
Test mode SSO connections behave differently from production — verify environment

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, GDPR compliant. OAuth2 for API access. TLS enforced. Webhook signature verification. Purpose-built for enterprise security requirements — SAML signing, encryption, and certificate management handled.
